trajectory.description: Observation: Baseline ResNet-50 DDP (10 epochs, lr=0.001, batch=96/GPU) achieved Quickdraw accuracy 0.0959, val_acc 0.6315. Loss still declining, validation accuracy improving. Domain imbalance (Real 172k vs Clipart 48k) likely biases the model. Analysis: Quickdraw extreme domain gap demands longer training, domain-aware weighting, and augmentations that mimic sketch-like inputs (grayscale). Decision: Scale to 30 epochs, lr=0.01 (standard finetuning), batch 128/GPU, add domain inverse-frequency loss weighting and RandomGrayscale augmentation. Submit eval in same iteration to save iteration budget.

trajectory.description: Observation: ViT-B/16 AugReg trained for 5 epochs (lr=0.01, AdamW, AMP) achieved 16.23% Quickdraw accuracy (up from 12.27% ResNet). Validation accuracy 71.0% and loss 1.06 show underfitting and room to improve. Analysis: ViT is clearly better; the high lr may be suboptimal for full-model fine-tuning; longer training with AdamW and lower lr (5e-5) should improve generalization. Decision: Run 30 epochs with lr=5e-5, weight_decay=0.01, batch_size=256 effective, AMP, CosineAnnealingLR. Submit eval immediately to measure maximum improvement. Expected ~100 min, well within budget.

trajectory.description: Observation: ViT-B/16 AugReg, 30 epochs lr=5e-5, val_acc improved to 73.4% but Quickdraw dropped to 15.45% (from 16.23% at 5 epochs lr=1e-4). Overfitting to source domains. Analysis: validation accuracy on mixed source domains does not predict Quickdraw performance; we need a better proxy (e.g., sketch accuracy on val set) and stronger regularization. Also other backbones (CLIP, DINOv2) may generalize better. Decision: Rewrite train.py with per-domain validation accuracy and save best checkpoint by sketch accuracy. Add RandAugment, RandomSolarize options. Train CLIP (ViT-B/16 from OpenAI) for 5 epochs (lr=1e-5) as baseline; submit eval. Then evaluate DINOv2 next.

trajectory.description: Observation: CLIP ViT-B/16 5-epoch baseline achieved 21.57% Quickdraw accuracy (best so far, beating published ~17%). Validation accuracy 79.73%, loss still declining at epoch 5 (0.85). Analysis: CLIP's vision-language pretrained features are far superior for the extreme Quickdraw domain gap; longer training with regularization (mixup, label smoothing, RandAugment) and per-domain validation tracking (saving best by sketch accuracy) could push higher. Decision: Train CLIP for 20 epochs with mixup=0.2, label_smoothing=0.1, RandAugment if available, DDP mixed precision, and save best checkpoint based on sketch validation accuracy. Expect ~77 min; well within time budget. Submit eval immediately after training to save iteration.

trajectory.description: Observation: iteration 7 (CLIP 20 epochs with mixup, label smoothing) achieved Quickdraw 19.5%, down from 21.6% (iteration 6, 5 epochs). Training curves show source-domain overfitting (val acc 81.2%, sketch acc 77.6%) while OOD performance dropped. Analysis: mixup and label smoothing are not helping DG; longer training overfits source domains. Need stronger OOD regularization and better checkpoint selection. Decision: train CLIP with SWAD (stochastic weight averaging densly) which finds flat minima known to improve DG; add heavy sketch‑mimicking augmentations (RandAugment, grayscale, solarize, color jitter) to bridge domain gap. Use 10 epochs, lr=1e‑5, no mixup/smoothing, SWAD start epoch 5. Submit eval immediately to check improvement.

Analysis: CLIP fine‑tuning with SWAD and extra augmentations did not improve OOD generalization; DINOv2 (SSL backbone, 86M) is a strong alternative proven in DG literature and hasn’t been tried. Additionally, an ensemble of the best CLIP checkpoint (0.2157) with a new DINOv2 model via logit averaging may boost accuracy further, as ensemble methods often yield improvements in domain generalization. Decision: train DINOv2 ViT‑B/14 for 10 epochs with lr=1e‑4 and AdamW (using existing train.py), preserve the best CLIP checkpoint, then submit eval using an ensemble predict.py that averages logits from both models. This combination could surpass the current best.

trajectory.description: Observation: Inspection shows checkpoint keys have 'backbone.' prefix matching model. Predict.py failed earlier due to missing state_dict extraction (checkpoint has 'state_dict' outer key). TTA (10-crop) can improve accuracy without new training. Time budget 2.2h, 8 iterations. Decision: rewrite predict.py to handle checkpoint loading robustly (extract state_dict if nested, strip 'module.' prefix), apply 10-crop TTA, lower batch_size to avoid OOM, and resubmit eval with existing best CLIP checkpoint. This should recover at least 0.2157 and likely surpass with TTA. No training needed now, saving time for potential DINOv2 later.
